nodes.name: OpenAPI-first HTTP contracts

nodes.name: Mixed MongoDB and MariaDB persistence
nodes.summary: Consulting type documents and application settings are MongoDB documents; topics and topic groups are MariaDB/JPA entities with Liquibase changelog history.

nodes.name: TenantContext request scoping
nodes.summary: When multitenancy is enabled, HttpTenantFilter resolves the tenant from a header or subdomain and stores it in TenantContext for repository filtering and entity stamping.

nodes.name: Role to authority mapping
nodes.summary: Keycloak roles such as tenant-admin, single-tenant-admin, and topic-admin are mapped to local AUTHORIZATION values used by PreAuthorize.

nodes.name: Startup seed loading
nodes.summary: ConsultingTypeLoader reads consulting-type-settings JSON at startup and persists documents into MongoDB using technical tenant context 0.

nodes.name: Consulting Type Catalog
nodes.summary: Owns consulting type settings, groups, slugs, tenant-specific variants, and downstream catalog contracts consumed by frontend, agency, and user services.

businessRules: consulting type id and slug must be unique within the active repository mode
single-domain limited patch users cannot change language formal or video-call settings

nodes.name: Topic Taxonomy
nodes.summary: Owns topic and topic group read/write APIs, multilingual conversion, feature gating, and tenant-scoped JPA access.

businessRules: topic admin operations require local authorization grants
topic admin APIs are blocked when the tenant topic feature is disabled

nodes.name: Application Settings
nodes.summary: Owns global application settings stored as a single MongoDB document and exposed through settings endpoints.

businessRules: there must be at most one application_settings document
default settings are inserted at startup when none exist

nodes.name: Security And Tenancy
nodes.summary: Combines stateless JWT authentication, role-authority conversion, CSRF header and cookie checks, tenant resolution, tenant filters, and outgoing tenant service calls.

businessRules: selected admin endpoints require specific authorization values
unresolved tenant requests are denied when tenant filtering is active

nodes.name: Consulting Type Read Flow
nodes.summary: REST requests for basic, extended, full, slug, tenant, and group views move from generated API contracts through controllers into services, repositories, and response mappers.

nodes.name: OpenAPI contract
nodes.summary: api/consultingtypeservice.yml defines public consulting type endpoints and operationIds.

nodes.name: Controller dispatch
nodes.summary: ConsultingTypeController implements the generated interface and dispatches to ConsultingTypeService or ConsultingTypeGroupService.

nodes.name: Repository mode
nodes.summary: ConsultingTypeRepositoryService is backed by tenant-aware or tenant-unaware Mongo repository implementations depending on multitenancy.

nodes.name: DTO mapping
nodes.summary: Basic, extended, full, and group mappers shape schema model data into generated response DTOs.

nodes.name: Startup Consulting Type Loading
nodes.summary: At application startup, JSON files from consulting-type-settings are loaded, optionally validated, converted to schema model objects, and inserted into MongoDB.

nodes.name: Seed JSON files
nodes.summary: consulting-type-settings JSON files provide local consulting type defaults.

nodes.name: PostConstruct loader
nodes.summary: ConsultingTypeLoader runs at startup and reads all JSON files from consulting.types.json.path.

nodes.name: Schema validation hook
nodes.summary: ConsultingTypeValidator is wired but validation is currently disabled.

nodes.name: Mongo write
nodes.summary: ConsultingTypeRepositoryService.addConsultingType persists the loaded consulting type documents.

nodes.name: Topic Admin Mutation Flow
nodes.summary: Topic admin endpoints require JWT authorization, feature-gate checks, validation, sanitization, conversion, and JPA writes.

nodes.name: JWT and method authorization
nodes.summary: SecurityConfig maps JWT roles; TopicAdminController methods require create or update topic authorization.

nodes.name: Topic feature gate
nodes.summary: TopicFeatureAssertionAspect calls TopicFeatureAuthorisationService before TopicAdminController methods.

nodes.name: Validate and sanitize input
nodes.summary: TopicServiceFacade calls TopicValidationService and TopicInputSanitizer before conversion.

nodes.name: Tenant-aware JPA write
nodes.summary: TopicService stamps timestamps and saves TopicEntity through TopicRepository.

nodes.name: Application Settings Flow
nodes.summary: Settings APIs read and patch a single MongoDB application_settings document with generated schema models and DTOs.

nodes.name: Default initializer
nodes.summary: DefaultApplicationSettingsInitializer inserts defaults when application_settings is empty.

nodes.name: Public settings read
nodes.summary: ApplicationSettingsController exposes GET /settings without authentication.

nodes.name: Admin patch
nodes.summary: PATCH /settingsadmin requires patch application settings authorization and updates mutable setting values.

nodes.name: Single-document invariant
nodes.summary: ApplicationSettingsService rejects more than one settings document.

nodes.name: Authentication And Tenant Resolution Flow
nodes.summary: Requests enter the stateless Spring Security chain, JWT claims are converted to local authorities, and optional tenant resolution populates TenantContext.

nodes.name: Stateless CSRF filter
nodes.summary: StatelessCsrfFilter checks header and cookie token equality for non-read requests except whitelisted paths.

nodes.name: JWT resource server
nodes.summary: SecurityConfig reads Keycloak JWT claims from realm_access and resource_access.

nodes.name: Role authority mapper
nodes.summary: RoleAuthorizationAuthorityMapper maps tenant-admin, single-tenant-admin, and topic-admin to local grants.

nodes.name: Tenant filter
nodes.summary: HttpTenantFilter resolves tenantId from header or subdomain when multitenancy is enabled.

nodes.name: Persistence scope
nodes.summary: TenantAspect enables Hibernate filters and TenantHibernateInterceptor stamps tenant-aware writes.

nodes.name: Deployment And Configuration Flow
nodes.summary: Build and runtime configuration connect Maven generation, Spring profile properties, datasource URLs, Keycloak and TenantService endpoints, and the Docker image.

nodes.name: Maven build
nodes.summary: pom.xml generates schema models, generated APIs, tenant client code, and the Spring Boot jar.

nodes.name: Spring properties
nodes.summary: application.properties and application-local.properties supply env-backed URLs, credentials, and toggles.

nodes.name: Docker runtime
nodes.summary: Dockerfile copies ConsultingTypeService.jar and starts it with JDWP enabled.

nodes.name: External service endpoints
nodes.summary: TenantServiceApiControllerFactory uses tenant.service.api.url to call ORISO TenantService.

nodes.name: topic
nodes.summary: MariaDB topic table mapped by TopicEntity; tenant-scoped with Hibernate tenantFilter and TopicRepository query methods.

nodes.name: topic_group
nodes.summary: MariaDB topic_group table mapped by TopicGroupEntity with many-to-many relationship to TopicEntity.

nodes.name: topic_group_x_topic
nodes.summary: Join table linking topic groups to topics.

nodes.name: consulting_types
nodes.summary: MongoDB collection storing consulting type documents generated from the consulting-type JSON schema.

nodes.name: application_settings
nodes.summary: MongoDB collection storing the single application settings document and dynamic release toggles.

tour.title: Start With The Contracts
tour.description: The API surface starts in api files and services/tenantservice.yaml; Maven turns those contracts into controller interfaces, DTOs, and a TenantService RestTemplate client.
tour.languageLesson: When changing endpoints, update the OpenAPI file first, then regenerate or compile so handwritten controllers continue implementing the contract.

tour.title: Read The HTTP Entrypoints
tour.description: The controller package is thin and mostly delegates to services or facades. This is the fastest way to understand supported client actions.
tour.languageLesson: Controllers in this repo implement generated interfaces; missing method implementations usually mean the OpenAPI contract changed.

tour.title: Understand Security Before Mutations
tour.description: JWT role extraction, local authority mapping, CSRF checks, and method-level authorization annotations decide which mutation paths are available.
tour.languageLesson: URL permit rules and method authorization must be reviewed together.

tour.title: Follow Consulting Type Data
tour.description: Consulting type data is seeded from JSON, represented by generated schema models, stored in MongoDB, and mapped to basic, extended, or full response DTOs.
tour.languageLesson: The repository implementation changes with multitenancy; debug TenantContext before assuming a Mongo query is global.

tour.title: Follow Topic Data
tour.description: Topic APIs route through TopicServiceFacade for validation, sanitization, conversion, and then to TopicService and JPA repositories.
tour.languageLesson: Topic writes are relational, not Mongo-backed; schema changes are in db/changelog and usually belong in ORISO-Database too.

tour.title: Check Settings And Feature Flags
tour.description: Application settings are a single MongoDB document initialized at startup and exposed through public and admin settings APIs.
tour.languageLesson: Several runtime features, including topic gating in single-domain mode, depend on settings values.

tour.title: Review Deployment Risks
tour.description: Build and runtime config is spread across Maven, application properties, Dockerfile, and environment variables.
tour.languageLesson: Treat pom.xml, Dockerfile, and application.properties as one deployment contract.
